column4: vandal-counter burn threshold for locations equals their blood cost; no section owns burn-threshold formulas for equipment/locations consumed by counters. Nearest 1.10 (burn mechanics) and 1.7 (the cost value being referenced), neither claims it.

cumulative/stacking effects: multiple graft counters give no additional effect (-1 stealth flat, not -X per counter). Nothing owns "stacking"; 1.2 nearest (periodicity/wording templates) but does not claim it. Watch-list gap, cf. Betrayer.3.

specific interaction between Dragos's own burn-cost ability and superior {Terror Frenzy}'s combat-card protection; fails transfer — only concerns this card pairing (and is explicitly a reversal of prior rulings, i.e. card-specific history, not a stable principle). Nearest 1.7, which doesn't own this pairing.

her own conditional vote-granting requirement (prey controls a ready Camarilla vampire); pure card-specific condition, no general principle about votes — fails transfer, any card can carry an arbitrary condition. Nearest 3.7.6.
her own condition restricting ability use to when she's in combat; card-specific, fails both tests — obviously any ability can be conditioned this way. Nearest 1.5 (ability conditions).
prohibits herself from playing one specific named other card ({Lay Low}); pure card-specific text, no transfer beyond this one pairing, and nothing general to state.
restates her own printed ability text (steal 2 blood or life); no interpretive content, purely descriptive of the card.
restates her own numeric stealth bonus condition (superior [PRE] used with {The Summoning}); card-specific, no general principle.
extremely specific three-card combo chain (second Jimmy Dunn + Vicissitude's {Reform Body} + unavoidable contest-burn); fails transfer — describes one scenario, not a restatable rule. Nearest 1.13.
same stacking gap as R2191: corruption counters don't multiply the damage (flat 1 regardless of count). Nothing owns cumulative/stacking; 1.2 nearest, doesn't claim it.
her own conditional stealth bonus (multi-target action, one or more targets lack a ready Sabbat vampire); card-specific text, no general principle.
his ability's own secrecy clause (replacement card not seen by opponent); card-specific, no general rule about replacement visibility transfers elsewhere. Nearest 1.9.
timing restriction relative to one specific other card ({Amaranth}); reads as a consequence of Amaranth's own diablerie/torpor effect removing him from play rather than a general rule. Nearest 3.7.8.
